summary: `auth status` now considers a user authenticated when AuthHeader() can produce a Bearer — i.e. AuthHeaderVal, EtherpadOpenid, or AccessToken — instead of only AccessToken.
reason: Greptile P1 on PR #396 final review: `authed := cfg.AccessToken != ""` did not cover the env-var ($ETHERPAD_OPENID → EtherpadOpenid) or static-header (auth_header → AuthHeaderVal) credential paths that AuthHeader() reads from. So users authenticating via $ETHERPAD_OPENID or a TOML-set auth_header saw `authenticated: false` and "Not authenticated" output even though every API call succeeded with a valid Bearer token.
validated_outcome: Inlines AuthHeader()'s precedence (auth_header > env openid > access token) without calling it (AuthHeader has a write side-effect on AuthSource that would overwrite the value Load() already set, e.g. flipping "config" → "oauth2" when an AccessToken from disk is present). auth status now reports authenticated for all three credential shapes.
